textByHash.925a6e8c89f153bdd33dbc001d461a5fcf6c7b51: What do you think you're doing, human?
textByHash.fcca63d61581ea786ba533f594ee4482562f1e61: You can admire them from back here!
textByHash.16744a994e81b540ad4b0c46830ec1682785c2c0: Your beliefs do not grant you a right to anything in Cam Torum, not to document things, and especially not to get yourself killed.
textByHash.6737ae9f3ae1adbe4328139f87e5d02e0bf89430: I don't want to give Fortis any excuses to come knocking on our door to investigate your untimely and idiotic demise.
textByHash.bb2641157291054bfc9fa7e72c5af6531907983e: Oh yes? And will your divine oath feed you in there? Light your way? Keep you safe from cave-ins and monsters?
textByHash.a4fb707ecbeaefd79e7f9fa676e2781c12a60c91: Sorry, but I'm busy with a different arrogant human. You'll have to wait in line.
textByHash.c310464843a9b3350993b75e13d045f5db4d4957: No! We've more than enough humans in Cam Torum with this blasted Fortis mandated dig! I don't care how many deals it secured, it's not worth all this trouble!
textByHash.ccb3fda8f2962dc7e97a9725f78b3b73e24e06f5: The one that leads to the aqueduct?! We'll have Fortis guards barging into the city if they catch wind...
textByHash.173da7dfc337e28e1584f79ef823b28cf491c03d: Cam Torum does not need help, much less that of any humans!
textByHash.8cc7a7ec601d67193ebf90b8f8e70f8956e9fe4e: Wake the off-duty guards and put them to work. I'll ensure they're paid handsomely.
textByHash.c557524f1df565986841ec60a591ac5f585f77e0: Argh! Get back to your post!
textByHash.69797c3b5cdd45ab8fb90f528c7458e4845e6f3d: Alright, fine! Human, you look like you've seen a fight or two. Deal with the escaped creature by the mountain outflow spring.
textByHash.931bcec3384d135b31c78e710826e79cc5105ef3: Have you killed that creature yet?
textByHash.5dd66e7c713eb4fffcf2b15a92d126f95cbdd998: It's up by the mountain outflow spring, north east of here. Get to it before it makes its way to the aqueduct!
textByHash.ab10c90265cb751247143ed911cfa7ade7350f08: A spirit of sorts. Something far older than anything your people have bothered to remember. In any case, a deal is a deal. You may enter the city of Cam Torum.
textByHash.ea9c786dc77c065494b33f9850d02ea47fcfee9f: Fine! If it'll make you stop pestering me, you can join...
textByHash.20d1b2728c73acbc12c13ea3bf486835a218d4fe: , he's your responsibility now. If either of you cause any trouble, you're both out!
textByHash.7d0b2d7129474141bac0ba95f35651239d4d414e: Well with the suitation outside dealt with, I now need to keep an eye on this expedition. Any mistakes made in these ruins could bring the ground out from beneath our city!
textByHash.424e2fa4e950a4ff6a10a060869f02400862949f: It might be if you don't know what you're dealing with. Your people often assume they do, which generally makes things worse.
textByHash.0ee3db96d383ef863b449fb5feb8faa4546537fe: The rest?! I'm not happy you're here either.
textByHash.9c054646cea9a4912056f8e996ebf95e938c7fd0: You'll have my gratitude when you finish you business in Cam Torum and leave.
textByHash.9eb8d93cdc18db1bc614ac8970622aad0077ed7a: As I say, the situation has been dealt with for now. Besides, the only reason that situation existed in the first place is because of these ruins.
textByHash.f7200ec6bf0775c0ce1a34fa58e1b2184ccbe3aa: At this point a smart person would call off this dig entirely, but as that doesn't seem set to happen, I'd say it's in our best interests we keep an eye on things.
textByHash.39b4d4ed5c3c1a9445e0c13677186146439997c5: It's not exactly that. You have your virtues, but you have short sight and even shorter memories, which can be very dangerous.
textByHash.9ef4ad3b2a776312d1abcfdd14dea6efd7c992c5: I suspect some of your people wouldn't be too pleased if we did. The truth isn't something that everyone desires.
textByHash.1a4130f0c1a944c7c1487f188a7ece4456e06c2f: This is an old place. Older even than much of what we dwarves remember.
textByHash.161be59a8570eb55e0289167a519f4344969831e: No, it's dangerous is what it is.
textByHash.07e9d57aa63fe35fd0e63135923962260c84a826: Now where is that fool priest?
textByHash.a46ce37861cda1ee04502a0609de396776825ce4: You see a picture of a creature eating the sun and instead of a warning, you see evidence of your god? You really are beyond saving.
textByHash.efefb42ee0e3eadda5d06908cc5ae433fdc4ddfe: We don't need to be deciphering inscriptions to know that we shouldn't be here. I mean look at that... whatever that floating thing is. This place is dangerous!
textByHash.791fc093316828d27a292a20b45ae29c05ab00e7: I don't care what your book says! Your book won't keep us safe from...
textByHash.ef4257a25ef54920f62007a7e3c88f7b36c074f0: The last time you humans did something like this...
textByHash.3fc9c2840993e077746b1c5458ed587203764daf: Keep this up and I'll have you kicked out of the...
textByHash.aa5340c12fcddd182b11340c0f8b72b79aba537b: How can you make those claims so confidently?
textByHash.bea99dcd1033ee705fe290e7b97a2209b6edf1b0: First they built the place, now they're sealed in it. These leaping assumptions are going to get us all killed!
textByHash.afe7d4d5dba05bd7957a1cb243e33a26031081c5: I will not allow you to put Cam Torum in danger just because you're curious!
textByHash.02837a063f8b865c5550378320f174c3910049bd: Hmm... Nahta will know what to do with these. , take them to him in Cam Torum's magic shop. Tell him I sent you.
textByHash.dff77498dcebeb5a1d7068e6f50bb264e853078c: Come back to me when you have space and I'll give you the talismans.
textByHash.1c9f5a7a98894bd684e3d73a9ac53b01f3c1457e: It's raining blood! It is clearly a depiction of death!
textByHash.14d9485a7a8e705d89f43d7cf6e84234bd73dd63: Cam Torum has no interest in bathing in blood!
textByHash.087031596d20d69bd33ae650bea3379649a4775a: Ximoua? It is eating the sun, you fool! If it's a herald of anything, it's death!
textByHash.06337082fc76f1ed0705d1687ea1b8628cd0d516: A creature snapping a mountain in half. Care to explain this one, Zuma?
textByHash.7510032320670f5aa9ed5700e2f5683dc7d990a4: You'll need to make some space for those two talismans .
textByHash.acc948ee225379ff0e93f62c8763c68aa1a60a52: Take the talismans to Nahta in Cam Torum's magic shop. Tell him I sent you.
textByHash.7f600751f7238b386cc59337d21db2459070d8d4: Then you'll need to find some more yourself, and take better care with them once you have!
textByHash.4772fb156ad010c396380ce8a2b309449a58830f: You're meant to be in Cam Torum sorting those talismans out. Return here once you have done so.
textByHash.aebf049296f712c70c76b1f37c5df8086547ad70: Good. Now let's not waste any more time. In theory, the talismans should guide you to the point in this place where these magics of earth and streams resonate most strongly.
textByHash.7ffbeb080b838a34adeeae12fbbf382da101d4e0: Of course, we've not tried something like this before, so I imagine it will require you to do some testing.
textByHash.f3e4f4904e304a1d42f12058af9ca9b9a7aa04fe: Of course. I am a key advisor to Highlord Katlo himself. Few dwarves hold as much sway as I do. Not just anyone can authorise the entry of a human.
textByHash.f2cc851ce41d1f4b7106f08c7926017b87afa736: Hmph! Don't make me regret it.
textByHash.ff2ef60d2339b3f01cd9a2cfdf3b214d8a506284: That doesn't surprise me. They're busy people with much less patience for humans than I have.
textByHash.e79ad08232b6b236adeac5a401e07904031e6e87: Have you managed to find the source of these earth and stream magics yet?
textByHash.81e152390c3a4029c626068bb2db48273bd81b38: Remember, the talismans should guide you to the point in this place where these magics resonate most strongly.
textByHash.01576d4eb161b460e539e0e83949ab3ed960b3f7: I knew I shouldn't have let that fool priest in!
textByHash.3f41af72a2b2dd24232b4999fa6c7c8be7f01305: After berating you about keeping an eye on the priest, I failed to do the same myself. I was caught up in the mystery.
textByHash.175af1d90b65005a46558e7e1a811995a2453275: Perhaps there's a little human in me after all!
textByHash.78efae278b1de47cc5a379f9b3888557c10d21a3: The nagua seems to have relaxed. I assume you were successful?
textByHash.ecaf563b50be2a3bdf4b08dca2e7ac783bdc544f: You have my gratitude. You have helped to ensure Cam Torum's safety. You will always be welcome back.
textByHash.0f2e7c08ca0660ce15bcca7d241d3c3bc719400a: Well it's not their choice, so they'll just have to suck it up. Who knows, maybe given long enough, you might start to grow on them!
textByHash.5bddfc2e2ff3cc638d520f8ad7fad5da3f1caf98: Hello there. What can I do for you?
textByHash.94d0890eae24eb44dedcb551941828e519da9ccf: Now that the situation in the ruins is under control, things are nice and quiet, which is just what we need. That whole buisness down there got a lot of dwarves riled up.
textByHash.e2006048393722380f66ffe8e51e7a69f3764ef9: Ha! Good one. You think I'm going to make a laughing stock of myself by daring to claim a human did something positive? Keep dreaming!
textByHash.c8df9b103b36c827ca601855b455d0f727068f1d: Hmph. Very well. The Varlamorians won't mention this, but we were the first to settle here. Our ancestors saw the writing on the wall when Xeric came to power in Kourend. They chose not to stick around.
textByHash.e11314055096478ba13a0fb9bcaf0c6a4524b057: Doubtful. My people are all too familiar with that sort of thing. The Lovakengj dwarves have mostly forgotten their history, but we Dwarves of Cam Torum remember it all too well.
textByHash.e6564c6df8c5309593ed22456b206fc0278c16a7: Before Lovakengj, there was Imcandoria. Our ancestors there were forced to abandon their home, just as we were forced to leave Kourend.
textByHash.0fac63726cfc6a3a770483c210308c2b1369b79e: If you humans had had your way, we might have had to abandon Cam Torum by now as well.
textByHash.5b7f6d7fad94e07ebfcec480f75b1bd73bce5867: But I do know that. As I said, we remember our history, and we know that those who don't are doomed to repeat it.
textByHash.1df83a4119a32286ba3d9bb946130b337fc9a4f1: The Varlamorians nearly destroyed themselves in their War of Betrayal, but it was not them who were betrayed, it was us.
textByHash.829cd46eab5a3c7239dda70381b8c422b359e615: I was a young dwarf when their war risked destroying our home. I saw the results of their pride. Meanwhile, they've all but forgotten it.
textByHash.2a4949e71fe06d28a849dee4a398dbec4b04ca37: Hmph. It's fine. Your intentions are good, , which is more than can be said for most of your kind. Who knows, maybe one or two dwarves here might even grow to tolerate you.
textByHash.1792d8fd8520d255036c82ee54822b135dd12cbf: If I may, Highlord, the human travelling with the Queen here is a friend to us. They are the one who helped us deal with the issues in the ruins beneath the city.
textByHash.6e8c1d872cb0bb9074db28fd57ae104dd38e8158: If it is this human who is to be conducting the investigations here, I am sure they will act with discretion.
textByHash.b09391419dc7dc4e86c72ff58abbb0c557a738dd: I will wait for you in the market. I have some ideas for where you might begin your search.
textByHash.8cc787df2e2c31741772f1f76d0a66bb3d41bd82: When I vouched for you back there, , I wasn't expecting you to bring a Teokan with you.
textByHash.b4c81a308d530f91792a32be0e8100f192089a7c: Hmph. Help with what exactly? You claim there are cultists in our city who want access this crypt of yours, but you haven't said why. What's so special about the crypt?
textByHash.bf65ddd23b871fb101fe4579c9f4b57ac3829b35: Ah, of course they do. Don't you humans ever tire of your fantasies?
textByHash.e1a6a94af9cd15e39a570977c606a0dbbc310c36: Ha! And yet here you are trying to stop some cultists from doing just that!
textByHash.915ca8abffd2c18e82710b1feece91cedc9aa87a: Ah, of course! A lying heathen. Just like us here, no doubt?
textByHash.2b9d9486b6c9fcd02e684f396f60b8bf16691dd5: Hmph. As the Highlord said, there are only four dwarves who can grant humans access to Cam Torum. They are myself, the Highlord, Highseer Vandra, and Teumo, Vandra's advisor.
textByHash.22dd8c1ed5a9841588417cde0da65c64aa90227c: Not exactly, but of those, Teumo is the most likely candidate. I trust the Highlord and the Highseer, and I know for a fact it wasn't me.
textByHash.93ea8abdfd9013d6dba6a97628112e676f2d8e27: His house is the one behind the bank, east of here. Let us meet there.
textByHash.240ef0390632373c7572503352ff3b9b52b21fd3: Believe me, the priest wasn't my choice.
textByHash.0d9ea0e4850b3126089534f27062d132ed842c21: Anyway, don't worry yourself, Galna. It's not an urgent matter by any means. It's just a little project that Teumo was working on that needs finishing off.
textByHash.c06d09692ab2ac7a9e4c7ad77ab6e7e421c20dff: Speaking of which, do you mind if we take a look around while we wait for him? I imagine he'll have left some things from his project here.
textByHash.740959a75d69f98ee1626422ed70901e778ee459: Is there anything we can get for you while we wait, Galna? Given we came barging in on you, it's the least we can do.
textByHash.65699ba3eb7306ccea2cbeaec15cd6465d55697d: Thanks again for letting us look around, Galna.
textByHash.51c89b686c97ea951f8809938605a2beb87065d0: Teumo must have had a reason for not wanting people down here. Let's find out what it is.
textByHash.894d7aaaae093d7eb3fd2f8e86279e1371243bbd: Interesting... Whatever you did caused the fire to go out...
textByHash.04106581e53dc80a722b290aba49234c4c789414: Well, just so I'm understanding correctly, there's a cult who believe there is a path to the centre of the world?
textByHash.0be1106c46fc07f3baffd41d73f9b9a576b81c99: And what do they intend to do once they get there?
textByHash.497f627f36dbd860daed0eba3b3ead703f4c16d9: Of course! What a totally normal thing to believe. Only humans would ever manage to reach this level of silliness.
textByHash.d6e667a78bbfeebcc1fa77d58e2e382e4d1286e7: No, you just believe a different nonsense. I must admit, to those of us who live in the real world, it can sometimes be hard to tell the difference.
textByHash.96668897b1659a53b6b5a3df7f7fcfc6f2fe4cd3: Anyway, as for my take, your cultists are going to end up disappointed. There is no Mokhaiotl. The only path they will find is one leading to their deaths.
textByHash.ac0509176f4545bfa26f4b916e8a7e1356fc3694: Really? How curious... Hard to know what that might mean though. Nagua are not known for being reliable.
textByHash.88c5bcb78ae0139c1888d7843e345ee0b9fcd865: The truth isn't something that ever seems to matter to your kind. This cult has already made up their minds on what they believe, just as your priest here has as well.
textByHash.b4533cecc963af8f441420eec7813156d8c0dfc7: That Ralos doesn't exist. That Ranul doesn't exist. That Mokhaiotl doesn't exist. It's as simple as that. Now, if that answer satisfies you, shall we get back to work?
textByHash.6d79b6b169b07d73572985a1a9a2c0e822de738d: More trouble in Neypotzli? Great! This is just what we need!
textByHash.4b9fb38a75497f16bbb6c7f12c6f30ed62200a92: I'll gather some guards and see if we can find Teumo. While I do that, , you should go to Neypotzli and find the cultists.
textByHash.dd0462326796236694509706d75931d51aa63778: There you are. That nagua told us how to find you.
textByHash.0351efb99acc603e0033e8621bc353ae0b8f7986: I see. That's not the only bad news, I'm afraid. We searched the whole city, but couldn't find any trace of Teumo. My guess is he's already left to go join those cultists.
textByHash.a071fc144a4f9a1966d7d1432344f2f68b5d968b: Sounds like you should head to Tal Teklan and report to your Queen then.
textByHash.7a254d26bde24352de48e1b93c003a473dd9d655: You know as well as I do, , these cultists are fools. There is no world ending threat here, nor any threat to Cam Torum. This is a human problem. It's for humans to deal with.
textByHash.bb1ad9fad24582ca73ee4fe55217d46789e0efc2: If it's any consolation, I hope you don't die. You're one of the few humans that I don't tire of interacting with. Now, I'd better go report to the Highlord.
textByHash.794bdf580ae765a10948c29bc50c808f42c08855: Hello there. I hear that cultist business of yours ended up getting sorted. Your Queen traded Teumo with the Highlord in return for a new mineral deal.
textByHash.3b94969ee8e601c4f39a2a4df9f11a254365d067: On that topic, I'm going to guess that they didn't end up finding the centre of the world?
textByHash.4e2432f3fc5c7e9a9ca0fd41fa99afa0b67e6b63: Ah, so once again you humans managed to misinterpret something the Old Ones left behind. Some things never change.
textByHash.abeb77cd34b65e9a92308d539baa61eae82a011e: Anyway, was there something you wanted?
textByHash.4e475544f1d3e6a864aaf6a94c66676b67c86cdf: Well, things were quiet for a while, but then you dug up that Teumo business. The Highlord and the Highseer were both rather unhappy. The Highseer in particular. She now needs to find a new advisor.
textByHash.b44dbdc88ec80826249c99b13be140aca28d04fd: Still, at least the world didn't end. Hard to imagine that some of you humans actually considered that a possibility! Your kind never change.
